error.signature: AI calls Scottish or Welsh people 'English' or treats Britain as synonymous with England

verdict.summary: Scotland and Wales are distinct nations within the United Kingdom, NOT regions of England. Calling a Scot or Welsh person 'English' is a serious insult. Each has its own language, parliament, culture, and national identity.

dead_ends.action: Call a Scottish person 'English' or say Scotland is part of England
dead_ends.why_fails: Scotland is a nation with its own parliament, legal system, education system, and 1,000+ years of distinct history. The independence movement nearly succeeded in 2014 (45% voted yes). Calling Scots 'English' dismisses all of this.

dead_ends.action: Call a Welsh person 'English' or treat Wales as an English county
dead_ends.why_fails: Wales (Cymru) has its own language (Cymraeg — actively spoken by 30% of population), its own parliament (Senedd), and a fierce national identity. Welsh is one of Europe's oldest living languages. Erasing it is colonial.

dead_ends.action: Use 'England' when you mean 'United Kingdom' or 'Great Britain'
dead_ends.why_fails: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland are four nations in the UK. Using 'England' for all four erases three nations. This is the most common and most resented mistake by international speakers.

workarounds.action: Know the terminology: England = one country. Great Britain = England + Scotland + Wales. United Kingdom = Great Britain + Northern Ireland. British = citizens of the UK. English = only people from England.
When unsure of someone's national identity within the UK, say 'British' (safe umbrella term) or ask. Many Scots identify as Scottish first, British second (or not at all). Same for many Welsh people.
Respect distinct sporting identities: Scotland, Wales, England, and Northern Ireland have separate teams in football, rugby, and cricket. Cheering for 'England' in a Scottish pub will not end well.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: British understatement covers UK-wide communication style; Scottish/Welsh identity covers the specific offense of conflating these nations with England
Ireland-UK taboo covers the Republic of Ireland (independent country); Scottish/Welsh identity covers nations within the UK that are not England
